name: DAXOR CORP
business: Daxor Corporation is a medical device manufacturing company which provides additional biotechnology services, such as cryobanking and frozen blood storage. The Company's major focus is the creation and development of the BVA-100 Blood Volume Analyzer, an instrument that rapidly and accurately measures human blood volume, used in conjunction with Volumex, a single-use radiopharmaceutical diagnostic injection and collection kit. The Company also offers cryobanking services for blood storage through Scientific Medical Systems and for semen storage through Idant.
sellsTo: approximately 8,500 hospitals and Radiology Imaging Centers in the United States
additional international market of 10,000-14,000 potential users

exposures.sentence: Daxor Corporation is a medical device manufacturing company which provides additional biotechnology services.
The BVA-100 is used to diagnose and treat patients with heart failure, kidney failure, hypertension and syncope, and to aid in fluid and blood transfusion management in the critical care unit.

catalysts.event: Completion of TEAM-HF Study enrollment (300 patients from 13 medical centers)
catalysts.sentence: The TEAM-HF (Treatment to Euvolemia/Normovolemia by Assessment and Measured Blood Volume in Heart Failure) Study will enroll a total of 300 patients from thirteen (13) participating medical centers.

catalysts.event: Completion of ultrafiltration study in heart failure patients (50 patients)
catalysts.sentence: Daxor is currently supporting a study which will determine whether use of blood volume measurement to help guide fluid removal by ultrafiltration (UF) in patients hospitalized with decompensated Heart Failure (HF) leads to improved outcomes; the 50 patients who enroll in this interventional study will undergo 4 blood volume measurements.

catalysts.event: Filing of two additional patent applications for a semi-automated instrument to measure human blood volume in March 2012
catalysts.sentence: The Company filed two additional patent applications for an automated instrument to measure human blood volume in March of 2012.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 8,500 hospitals and Radiology Imaging Centers in the United States as a market opportunity
tamClaims.sentence: The Company believes that the most significant market for its blood volume measurement equipment consists of the approximately 8,500 hospitals and Radiology Imaging Centers in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Additional international market of 10,000-14,000 potential users
tamClaims.sentence: The Company believes that there is an additional international market of 10,000-14,000 potential users of the BVA-100.

tamClaims.claim: $38 billion spent each year on heart failure treatment, of which $23 billion is spent on hospital treatment
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that $38 billion is spent each year on heart failure treatment, of which $23 billion is spent on hospital treatment.
